top_signals.title: First Saudi Supertankers Start Leaving Hormuz After Deal
top_signals.summary: Three Saudi supertankers were among a growing list of vessels exiting the Strait of Hormuz in an early sign of rising flows after the US and Iran’s interim peace deal, although some trade groups warned that their members needed more clarity on mines and other practicalities.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: More Than 60 Million Barrels of Oil Ready to Head to Asia as Hormuz Reopens
top_signals.summary: Crude cargo arrivals in Asia from the Middle East could accelerate in the coming weeks as more than 60 million barrels of oil stuck in the Persian Gulf prepare to exit the Strait of Hormuz and head to Asian markets once the chokepoint reopens to traffic.   About 62 million barrels of crude oil on nearly three dozen supertankers are expected to make their way to Asia within weeks after the Strait reopens, according to Signal Group data carried by Bloomberg. Asia, which felt the supply shock first and the most as early as in March, could now…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
